purpose: Number adjacent to variable becomes (* N var).
Single digit adjacent to variable becomes multiplication.
Hex literal adjacent to variable becomes multiplication.
Single hex digit adjacent to variable becomes multiplication.
Negative number adjacent to variable becomes multiplication.
Binary literal adjacent to variable becomes multiplication.
Octal literal adjacent to variable becomes multiplication.
Repeating single digit decimal becomes exact ratio.
Repeating multi-digit decimal becomes exact ratio.
Repeating two-digit decimal becomes exact ratio.
Integer part with repeating decimal becomes exact ratio.
Postfix brackets become function call (arr index).
Postfix two-index becomes two-arg call on symbol.
Postfix parenthesized args become function call.
Dot field access on expression parses.
Left arrow becomes set! and returns value.
Single star expands to Pointer :: Type.
Double star expands to Pointer :: Pointer :: Type.
Unicode char adjacent to arg becomes prefix call.
Greek prefix adjacent to arg is prefix call.
Lambda literal desugars to lambda expression.
Cond with bracket syntax desugars to nested if.
Cond clause with multi body wraps in begin.
Cond with => calls test as function and applies.
Let* desugars to nested single-binding lets.
Empty heap array literal marked with is_heap flag.
Ampersand adjacent to symbol is address-of.

# name: # NOTE: ../parent/dir paths are not supported -- the lexer parses '..' as
# TOK_DOTDOT (range operator). Only ~/, /, and ./ prefixes trigger path literals.

purpose: [16kb I32] desugars to sized I32 array.
[16kb I64] desugars to sized I64 array.
[4kb I16] desugars to sized I16 array.
Wisp for with -> forward iteration desugars.
Wisp for with <- backward iteration desugars.

source: if cond then then-branch else else-branch

purpose: Wisp where clause binds local values.
source: define f x -> result where result = x + 1

purpose: Wisp where clause with function binding.
source: define sqrt x -> good x / 2 where good g = g * g

purpose: Wisp arrow clause desugars to lambda.
Wisp arrow clause with pattern matching.
Wisp def shorthand desugars to define.

# name: # NOTE: Guard syntax (define f x | cond -> body) is defined in the reader but
# the wisp expander does not yet handle | as a guard separator in define forms.
# These entries are reserved for when guard support lands in wisp output.

purpose: Infix a + b rewrites to (+ a b).
Infix a - b rewrites to (- a b).
Infix a / b rewrites to (/ a b).
Pipe cons desugars to (cons x xs).
Active #+ feature conditional keeps inner expr.
Inactive #+ feature conditional strips inner expr.
